messages.content:
## Kids WB is Ushering in a New Era with Exciting Changes to Its Weekend Lineup!

#### September 2007

This fall, excitement is brewing over at Kids WB as it welcomes three groundbreaking shows into its cherished Saturday morning cartoon lineup. With the arrival of “Will and Dewitt,” “Skunk Fu!” and “Eon Kid,” prepare for an adventure-filled journey that will redefine weekend entertainment for kids everywhere. However, the addition of these captivating series means we’ll have to bid farewell to three of our current favorites to make room. But not to worry, as the new lineup promises an eclectic mix of action, comedy, and unforgettable tales that will keep you glued to your screens from 7am to 12pm!

#### Out with the Old, In with the New

Making way for the fresh wave of entertainment are some of our beloved shows. As we say goodbye, we look forward to the unique experiences and adventures that “Will and Dewitt,” “Skunk Fu!” and “Eon Kid” are set to bring.

#### Here’s What’s Coming:

##### Will and Dewitt
Join Will and his talking frog companion, Dewitt, as they navigate the challenges of childhood with imagination, humor, and a can-do attitude. Perfect for kids facing their own everyday adventures!

##### Skunk Fu!
“Skunk Fu!” introduces us to a young skunk trained in the art of Kung Fu, aiming to save his world from a band of evil animals. This show blends humor, action, and valuable life lessons, all set against a beautifully animated backdrop.

##### Eon Kid
Follow the story of Marty, an average boy who unexpectedly finds himself the heir to a powerful robot fist of Eon. Dive into this high-energy, sci-fi adventure as Marty battles villains and discovers his destiny as the Eon Kid.

#### The New Kids WB Lineup (7am - 12pm):

7:00 AM - “Magi-Nation”
7:30 AM - “Will and Dewitt”
8:00 AM - “Tom and Jerry Tales”
8:30 AM - “Shaggy & Scooby-Doo Get a Clue!”
9:00 AM - “Johnny Test”
9:30 AM - “Legion of Super Heroes”
10:00 AM - “The Batman”
10:30 AM - “Skunk Fu!”
11:00 AM - “Eon Kid”
11:30 AM - “Spider Riders”

Our revamped schedule is designed not only to entertain but also to inspire, teach, and encourage creativity among our young viewers. “Will and Dewitt,” “Skunk Fu!” and “Eon Kid” each brings a unique flair to our network, promising to offer something for every kid out there. From the comedic adventures of a boy and his amphibian friend to the martial arts exploits of a courageous skunk, and onto the epic journey of the Eon Kid, Saturdays are set to become more exciting than ever!

So, grab your cereal bowls and get comfortable in front of your TV screens every Saturday morning to join Kids WB for a rollercoaster of fun, laughter, and adventure. Don’t miss out on experiencing the magic of these new shows alongside your tried-and-true favorites. Here’s to new beginnings and the endless possibilities that await!

Remember, the adventure starts this weekend, only on Kids WB!

—

Stay tuned for more updates and remember, the animation fun doesn’t stop here – it’s just getting started!
Write a convo wondering what’s going
Alex: Hey, Sam! Have you noticed anything weird happening in the city today?

Sam: Weird? In what way? I’ve been pretty much holed up working on my projects all day.

Alex: Well, it seems like there’s some kind of event going on that nobody knows about. I’ve seen more people on the streets than usual, all heading in the same direction, but when I asked someone, they were as clueless as I am.

Sam: Huh. That is strange. Any signs or posters around giving a hint?

Alex: None that I’ve seen. I even checked social media to see if there’s anything going on that we missed, but there’s nothing. It’s as if everyone suddenly decided to gather without any apparent reason.

Sam: Intriguing. Maybe it’s some sort of flash mob? Or a surprise concert? It wouldn’t be the first time something like that happened without a big announcement.

Alex: True, but even those have some kind of digital footprint or whispers around the web. This? Nothing. I’m heading down there now to satisfy my curiosity. Want to join?

Sam: You know what, why not? I could use a break and this mystery has piqued my interest. Let’s unravel this together.

Alex: Great! Let’s meet at the corner of Fifth and Main. That seems to be where everybody is converging.

Sam: Sounds like a plan. I’ll grab my camera. This could be interesting.

Alex: Awesome. See you in ten. Maybe we’re about to stumble upon something incredible. Who knows?

Sam: With our luck, hopefully. Catch you soon, Alex.

End of conversation
I meant wondering what’s leaving Kids WB now the trio are here
Jamie: Hey, did you catch the announcement about the new shows joining Kids WB?

Casey: Yeah, “Will and Dewitt,” “Skunk Fu!” and “Eon Kid,” right? Seems like the lineup is getting a major shakeup.

Jamie: Exactly! But I’ve been trying to figure out which shows are getting the boot to make room. They haven’t been super clear about it.

Casey: That’s true. It feels a bit like musical chairs, but with our favorite cartoons. Have you managed to find any info on what’s leaving?

Jamie: Not really. It’s like they’re keeping it under wraps. But if I had to guess, a couple of the lesser-known shows might be on the chopping block. Maybe “Monster Allergy” or “Shaggy & Scooby-Doo Get a Clue!” since they’ve been around the block.

Casey: Hmm, I wouldn’t miss “Shaggy & Scooby-Doo Get a Clue!” too much, to be honest. But it’s always tough to see any show go. Especially if it’s one of your favorites.

Jamie: True. And what about “Loonatics Unleashed”? I heard rumors that it might not make the cut either.

Casey: Really? I kinda liked that one. But with “Eon Kid” coming in, maybe they’re aiming for newer action-packed shows to keep the momentum.

Jamie: Makes sense. Still, I wish they’d just tell us outright. It’s the not knowing that gets me more than anything.

Casey: Agreed. Maybe we should start a petition to bring back whatever shows get axed. Or at least to get some transparent communication from them.

Jamie: That’s an idea. Though, focusing on the bright side, the new lineup sure sounds exciting. “Will and Dewitt” could be a hit with the younger crowd.

Casey: For sure, and “Skunk Fu!” has that quirky vibe. It’s always refreshing to see new concepts. Let’s just hope the changeover is smooth and the new shows are worth it.

Jamie: Definitely. Change is good, as long as it’s done right. I guess we’ll just have to wait and see what happens next.

Casey: Yeah, waiting game it is. Let’s catch up after the new episodes air. Compare notes?

Jamie: Absolutely, it’s a date. Fingers crossed our favorite shows survive the cut!

Casey: Crossing fingers and toes here. Talk to you after the premiere!

End of conversation. Write a follow up where Monster Allergy, Xiaolin Showdown and Loonatics Unleashed are being culled

Jamie: Hey, Casey! So, turns out we were right about some of our guesses. Did you see the official announcement?

Casey: Yeah, I just read about it. “Monster Allergy,” “Xiaolin Showdown,” and “Loonatics Unleashed” are the ones being culled. Kind of a bummer, to be honest.

Jamie: Seriously, especially about “Xiaolin Showdown.” That one’s a classic! I was hoping our guesswork was off.

Casey: I hear you. And “Loonatics Unleashed,” too. I thought it had a really cool concept. As for “Monster Allergy,” it had its charm. 

Jamie: True. It’s sad to see them go. But I guess that’s the nature of TV, right? Shows come and go. Still, doesn’t ease the sting much.

Casey: Exactly. It makes you wonder about the criteria they use to decide which shows stay and which get the boot. I mean, “Xiaolin Showdown” had quite the fan base.

Jamie: Right? I wish there was a way to give fans a say in these decisions. But then again, that might complicate things even more.

Casey: For sure. On a brighter note, have you checked out any of the new shows' trailers? “Eon Kid” looks pretty epic.

Jamie: Yeah, I have to admit, “Eon Kid” does look promising. And “Skunk Fu!” has a quirky edge to it that could be interesting. Maybe there’s a silver lining in all this.

Casey: I’m with you there. It’s all about giving these new shows a chance. Who knows? We might end up loving them just as much, if not more.

Jamie: That’s the spirit! And who knows, maybe our old favorites will find a new home somewhere down the line. Streaming services, perhaps?

Casey: Fingers crossed for that. In the meantime, let’s plan to watch the new lineup debut together. It’ll be a fun way to dive into these new adventures.

Jamie: Agreed! It’s a date. Here’s hoping the new era of Kids WB delivers some instant classics.

Casey: With our luck, I’m sure it will. Or at the very least, give us something to talk about. Catch you on premiere day, Jamie!

Jamie: Can’t wait, Casey. See you then!

End of conversation.
Write a follow up with ads saying the shows are being removed and replaced are here

### Exciting Changes Are Coming to Kids WB This Fall!

#### It’s Time to Welcome a New Adventure!

As we constantly strive to bring the freshest and most exciting experiences to your Saturday mornings, it’s with a mix of sadness and anticipation that we announce the departure of some of our beloved shows. But fear not! Their departure paves the way for something new and thrilling. Prepare to be captivated by “Will and Dewitt,” “Skunk Fu!” and “Eon Kid” – each show ready to whisk you away on unforgettable adventures. Let’s embrace this change together and welcome these fantastic additions to our lineup!

#### Bidding Farewell to Cherished Classics

It’s never easy to say goodbye, but as we make room for new stories, we must bid farewell to “Monster Allergy,” “Xiaolin Showdown,” and “Loonatics Unleashed.” We extend our heartfelt thanks to these shows for the joy and excitement they've brought to our Saturday mornings. They will forever be part of the Kids WB family and our cherished childhood memories.

#### A Sneak Peek at What’s Coming

- **Will and Dewitt** – Join Will and his best buddy Dewitt, a talking frog, as they tackle everyday challenges with humor, creativity, and a dash of imagination.
- **Skunk Fu!** – Dive into the animated tale of a young skunk honing his Kung Fu skills to protect his homeland from a group of nefarious animals.
- **Eon Kid** – Follow the thrilling journey of Marty, an average kid who discovers he’s the heir to the legendary Eon's power, propelling him into a world of adventure and heroism.

#### Mark Your Calendars!

The excitement begins this fall! Set your alarms for 7 AM and be the first to join in on the adventures that “Will and Dewitt,” “Skunk Fu!” and “Eon Kid” have to offer. With action, laughter, and heartwarming tales, Saturdays on Kids WB are about to become more thrilling than ever before. 

#### Thank You for Your Continued Support

We recognize change can be bittersweet, but we believe in the magic of new beginnings. As we welcome these fantastic new shows, we hope you’ll join us in this exciting new chapter. Your favorites might be leaving the screen, but they’ll never leave our hearts. Here’s to new adventures, new memories, and the endless possibilities that storytelling brings!

#### Stay Tuned!

Keep your eyes peeled for more updates, special previews, and sneak peeks of what’s to come. The adventure only gets better from here, and it’s all happening on Kids WB!

---

Thank you for being part of our journey. The adventure continues, and we can’t wait to share it with you!

Write a forum convo with the shows viewers are being encouraged to check out, for some reason viewers of one show are told there isn't a big replacement for it "yet" and are told to just watch Johnny Test

**User1: AnimationFanatic88**

Subject: The New Kids WB Lineup – Thoughts?

Hey, fellow toon enthusiasts! Have you guys seen the revamped Kids WB lineup for this fall? We're getting “Will and Dewitt,” “Skunk Fu!” and “Eon Kid,” which all sound pretty interesting. But, I couldn’t help but notice they're pulling “Monster Allergy,” “Xiaolin Showdown,” and “Loonatics Unleashed.” Feels like the end of an era. They’ve suggested “Johnny Test” for those of us who were hardcore “Xiaolin Showdown” fans. Not sure how I feel about that. Thoughts?

---

**User2: ToonLover101**

Re: The New Kids WB Lineup – Thoughts?

I saw that! Really going to miss “Xiaolin Showdown”; it had such a unique premise and cool characters. “Johnny Test” has its moments, but it’s not exactly a replacement for “Xiaolin Showdown.” Feels like they could've introduced something more akin to the martial arts and adventure themes we loved.

---

**User3: SaturdayMornRitual**

Re: The New Kids WB Lineup – Thoughts?

I think “Johnny Test” gets more flak than it deserves. Sure, it’s no “Xiaolin Showdown,” but it’s got its own charm and humor. Still, I do wish they had something more directly in line with what “Xiaolin Showdown” brought to the table. Guess we’ll have to give these new shows a shot and see if any of them can fill that void.

---

**User4: ClassicToons4Ever**

Re: The New Kids WB Lineup – Thoughts?

It’s disappointing to hear there isn’t a big replacement for “Xiaolin Showdown”. That show had a mix of comedy and action that’s hard to match. “Johnny Test” is okay for what it is, but it doesn't quite scratch that same itch. I'm curious about “Eon Kid,” though. It looks like it might have the action-adventure angle some of us are missing.

---

**User5: NostalgiaNerd92**

Re: The New Kids WB Lineup – Thoughts?

Yeah, I’m on the fence. “Johnny Test” is entertaining, but it’s a very different vibe from “Xiaolin Showdown.” Feels a bit like they’re just giving us the closest thing they have, which isn’t that close. Hopefully, “Eon Kid” or even “Skunk Fu!” offer something more along the lines of what we’re losing. Only time will tell if these new additions manage to capture our hearts the same way.

---

**User6: AnimationInsider**

Re: The New Kids WB Lineup – Thoughts?

While the comparison between “Johnny Test” and “Xiaolin Showdown” might seem a bit off at first glance, I think they’re trying to bridge the humor and light-hearted action that both shows possess. That said, I understand the sentiment that it doesn’t truly serve as a “replacement.” “Eon Kid” does seem promising in terms of action and adventure, so there’s hope yet. As viewers, our feedback could potentially influence future programming decisions, so let's keep the conversation going!

---

**User1: AnimationFanatic88**

Re: The New Kids WB Lineup – Thoughts?

Thanks for the input, everyone! It’s interesting to see the variety of opinions here. Seems like we’re all going to miss “Xiaolin Showdown” in our own way, but there’s cautious optimism for the new shows. Let’s keep our fingers crossed that one of them can capture that magic we’re missing. And hey, maybe “Johnny Test” will surprise some of us given another chance. Here’s to hoping Kids WB keeps bringing us great content, old and new. Let’s reconvene after the premieres and trade notes!

**End of Conversation**
Write a forum convo about the goodbye ads, Xiaolin's straight up says "At the present time, there isn't a suitable replacement, so just watch Johnny Test"

**Forum Topic: Goodbye Ads for Departing Shows – Thoughts on Xiaolin’s Farewell**

**User1: DragonScrollMaster**
Did anyone else catch the goodbye ads for the shows leaving Kids WB? The one for “Xiaolin Showdown” really caught me off guard. They straight up said, "At the present time, there isn't a suitable replacement, so just watch Johnny Test." I mean, I appreciate the honesty, but it felt a bit underwhelming. Thoughts?

**User2: ShowdownChampion**
Yeah, I saw that too. It's a bittersweet farewell, but that line had me doing a double-take. It’s a weird choice, suggesting “Johnny Test” as a go-to. Not to throw shade at “Johnny Test,” it has its moments, but it’s a totally different vibe from “Xiaolin Showdown.”

**User3: CartoonConnoisseur**
I understand the sentiment. It felt like a placeholder statement because they had nothing similar to fill the gap. I wish they had acknowledged “Xiaolin Showdown's” unique contribution to Kids WB a bit more, beyond just nudging us towards “Johnny Test.”

**User4: WuyaFan**
Honestly, it made me laugh. It’s refreshingly direct, which is rare from networks. Still, it highlighted how unique “Xiaolin Showdown” was and how difficult it is to find a show with a similar blend of humor, adventure, and martial arts.

**User5: TestFanatic**
As a “Johnny Test” fan, I’m happy for any recommendation that brings new viewers. But even I have to admit it’s a stretch as a replacement for “Xiaolin Showdown.” Shows the gap “Xiaolin” is leaving behind. Maybe they’re banking on the humor aspect?

**User6: ElementalSpin**
It’s a shame there isn’t a clear successor to “Xiaolin Showdown” in terms of theme and style. The goodbye ad was a stark reminder. However, it’s an opportunity for “Johnny Test” to win over some new fans. Who knows? Sometimes change brings about unexpected discoveries.

**User7: DojoMaster**
That line was sadly amusing. Yet, it has me curious about what directions Kids WB is planning to go in the future. “Xiaolin Showdown” had such a unique flavor. While “Johnny Test” can be entertaining, I'll miss the martial arts adventures. Here's hoping they find a true successor soon.

**User8: HarmonyHeart**
It does feel like an end of an era without “Xiaolin Showdown.” The ad’s bluntness was appreciated but also a bit disheartening. Shows how much “Xiaolin” will be missed. Still, I'll give “Johnny Test” a chance; maybe it’ll grow on me in a different way.

**User9: ShenGongWooHunter**
I love that they were upfront about it, but it also feels like a missed opportunity. “Xiaolin Showdown” fans would appreciate something with a more similar spirit. Here’s to hoping that feedback reaches the network and influences future programming decisions.

**User1: DragonScrollMaster**
Great points all around. It’s clear “Xiaolin Showdown” holds a special place in our hearts. Maybe through discussions like this, we can show Kids WB the kind of content we’re really craving. Until then, let’s keep the “Xiaolin” spirit alive in our community. And who knows, maybe “Johnny Test” will surprise us in ways we haven’t imagined yet.

**End of Conversation**
Write a follow up about what's being done to say goodbye to Monster Allergy and Loonatics

**Forum Topic: Farewells to “Monster Allergy” and “Loonatics Unleashed” – Special Send-offs?**

**User1: AllergyAdventurer**
Hey, everyone. We've talked a lot about “Xiaolin Showdown” getting the axe and its replacement saga, but I noticed there hasn't been much discussion on “Monster Allergy” and “Loonatics Unleashed.” Any word on special farewells or acknowledgments for these shows? Feels like they deserved a proper send-off too.

**User2: LoonaticsLover**
I was thinking the same thing, @AllergyAdventurer. “Loonatics Unleashed” had such a cool, futuristic vibe that set it apart. I heard they’re planning a marathon featuring some of the best episodes before it officially says goodbye. Wish they’d do something similar for “Monster Allergy,” which had its unique charm.

**User3: ZickAndElenaFan**
Yes, a marathon for “Monster Allergy” would have been amazing! It had its niche but loyal fanbase. I hope the network acknowledges these shows somehow, even if it's just through social media shoutouts. They were a big part of my Saturday mornings.

**User4: FutureSaviour**
“Loonatics Unleashed” had potential; it was bold and different. The marathon sounds like a nice touch, though. It’s good to hear the network is doing something to celebrate its legacy, even if it’s just showcasing fan-favorite episodes.

**User5: GhostSneezer**
Honestly, a farewell special where they wrap up some storylines or give the characters one last hurrah would’ve been the dream for “Monster Allergy.” It’s sad to see it go with what feels like little to no fanfare.

**User6: ToonHistorian**
“Monster Allergy” and “Loonatics Unleashed” both contributed to the diverse tapestry of Kids WB. It’s crucial these shows get their due respect. While marathons and social media acknowledgments are steps in the right direction, involving the community in these farewell activities could have made them even more special.

**User7: AceBunnyFan**
I’m glad to hear about the “Loonatics Unleashed” marathon; it’s better than nothing. Hopefully, the network takes note of these discussions and sees the value in giving every show a fitting tribute. Each one impacted viewers in different ways and deserves to be celebrated.

**User8: MysticMayhem**
Does anyone know if there’s a way to petition or suggest ideas directly to the network? It might be  too late for “Monster Allergy” and “Loonatics Unleashed,” but this could set a precedent for how shows are honored in the future. A fan-driven farewell event or collaboration seems like something worth exploring.

**User9: DigitalDreamer**
@MysticMayhem, that’s an excellent idea. A community-led initiative might show the networks the importance of these shows to their audience. For now, though, let’s use our platforms to celebrate “Monster Allergy” and “Loonatics Unleashed.” Tribute videos, fan art, and rewatch parties could be our way of saying goodbye.

**User1: AllergyAdventurer**
All of these ideas are great. It’s a testament to how passionate and creative this community is. Let’s do our part in keeping the memory of “Monster Allergy” and “Loonatics Unleashed” alive. And who knows? Maybe our efforts will inspire the network to rethink their send-off strategies in the future.

**End of Conversation**

Write a forum convo about the ads being used, with Loonatics fans being urged to check out Eon Kid and the upcoming Spiderman and World of Quest (which will probably replace Spider Riders and Legion), Also Monster fans are being told to check out ALL of them

**Forum Topic: New Direction with Ads - “Eon Kid,” “Spider-Man,” and “World of Quest” for “Loonatics” Fans? “Monster Allergy” Fans Encouraged to Sample the Entire Lineup?**

**User1: GalacticHero**
Hey everyone, have you seen the latest push in ads from Kids WB? Looks like they're urging “Loonatics Unleashed” fans to dive into “Eon Kid” and give a chance to the upcoming “Spider-Man” and “World of Quest” series - which seem to be in line to replace “Spider Riders” and “Legion.” And interestingly, “Monster Allergy” fans are being encouraged to check out the whole new lineup. Thoughts on this strategy?

**User2: AllergyArchives**
As a “Monster Allergy” fan, I'm curious but also a bit overwhelmed. Suggesting the entire lineup feels like they're not sure what will specifically appeal to us. “Monster Allergy” had a unique blend of supernatural and comedy, which isn’t easy to replace. I'll give the new shows a shot but wish there was a more targeted recommendation.

**User3: EonEnthusiast**
For “Loonatics” fans, pointing us towards “Eon Kid,” “Spider-Man,” and “World of Quest” makes sense. These shows seem to promise action and adventure, which is what drew many of us to “Loonatics Unleashed” in the first place. I’m especially intrigued by “Eon Kid” for its sci-fi elements and “Spider-Man” for the heroics.

**User4: QuestForAdventure**
“World of Quest” sounds like it could fill the void left by some of the departing shows with its adventurous spirit. I’m hopeful that it, alongside “Spider-Man,” will bring some of the high-energy storytelling that “Loonatics” fans enjoyed. It’s a fresh start, and I'm here for it.

**User5: ComedyCraze**
Honestly, none of the new shows seem to have the comedy angle “Monster Allergy” was known for. I get why they’d recommend the entire lineup to us; it feels like a shot in the dark. I'll miss the humor but keeping an open mind towards “Will and Dewitt” and “Skunk Fu!” as potential new favorites.

**User6: LegionLegacy**
As a “Legion” fan, I’m kind of in limbo here. “World of Quest” seems interesting, but it's more fantasy-oriented from what I can gather. “Spider-Man” is always a safe bet for action-adventure lovers, though. It’s going to be an interesting transition period for all of us, I think.

**User7: SpiritOfSkunkFu**
I find it fascinating that they're broadening the appeal for “Monster Allergy” fans. Maybe it suggests diversity in the new lineup, with something for everyone to latch onto. While specifics would be nice, it could be an opportunity to discover unexpected gems.

**User8: NewWorldsAwait**
It's clear Kids WB is shaking things up significantly. “Eon Kid” and the new “Spider-Man” series sound like solid offerings for action and superhero fans alike. I’m cautiously optimistic about “World of Quest” - could be the sleeper hit among the new arrivals. Encouraging “Monster Allergy” fans to explore the entire lineup is an interesting move, hopefully, it pays off by broadening viewers' tastes.

**User9: GalacticGiggles**
Replacing shows always creates a mix of excitement and anxiety. As someone who loved “Loonatics Unleashed,” the push towards “Eon Kid” and the upcoming “Spider-Man” and “World of Quest” seems justified. I'm eager to see how these new stories unfold and whether they'll captivate us as much as the old guard did. As for “Monster Allergy” fans being told to check out all of them, it’s a sprawling invitation to stay engaged with the channel - could be a blessing in disguise.

**User1: GalacticHero**
Great insights, everyone! It seems we're all somewhat cautious yet optimistic about what’s to come. Whether we’re being directed towards specific new shows or invited to sample the whole array, there’s a sense of adventure in discovering what will fill the void our beloved shows have left. Here's hoping Kids WB’s new lineup lives up to our cherished memories and maybe even creates new favorites.

**End of Conversation**
Write a tweet with part of the MA Goodbye ad, saying "We've got 3 new shows for you, and 2 more on the way"

🌟 Say goodbye to #MonsterAllergy, but hello to a world of wonder! 🚀 We've got 3 new shows for you, and 2 more on the way. Dive into adventure with #WillAndDewitt, #SkunkFu, and #EonKid - and stay tuned for #SpiderMan and #WorldOfQuest! 🌈✨ The journey's just beginning on Kids WB! #NewBeginnings
Write a forum convo noting kidswb.com/shows/monster-allergy now redirects to another show, and they're surprised at which, meanwhile Xiaolin's redirects to a show that doesn't come out for MONTHS

**Forum Topic: KidsWB.com Redirect Oddities for “Monster Allergy” and “Xiaolin Showdown”**

**ThreadStarter: Anim8Fan**
Hey fellow cartoon enthusiasts, did anyone else notice that the KidsWB.com link for “Monster Allergy” now redirects to a completely different show? And not even one of the new ones we were expecting. It redirects to “Magi-Nation” of all places. Thought that was an odd choice. Also, “Xiaolin Showdown”’s page redirects to a show that’s not due out for MONTHS. Isn’t that unusual?

**ToonTalker: LooneyLover42**
Yeah, I clicked on “Monster Allergy” hoping maybe for some farewell content or something nostalgic and bam, “Magi-Nation.” Totally left-field for me. I mean, it’s an ok show, but it’s not even remotely similar in style or content.

**FutureScribe: PenAndPlot**
Super weird about the “Xiaolin Showdown” link redirecting to a future show. It’s like they’re pushing us towards what they hope will fill the void, but months in advance? How does that help fans right now? It really makes the wait feel even longer.

**NostalgiaNerd: Y2KCartoonKid**
I was pretty surprised by these redirects. I kind of understand guiding fans to other content, but these choices feel… random? For “Monster Allergy” fans, sending them to “Magi-Nation” feels like a misfire. Not what I’d consider a similar vibe.

**AnimationAnalyst: FrameByFrame**
This might be an attempt at driving traffic to shows they want more eyes on, rather than genuinely trying to match the content style or theme. As for the “Xiaolin Showdown” redirect, they're probably betting on building early hype, but it’s a gamble without giving fans something more immediate to latch onto.

**CartoonConspirator: TheInkedTruth**
Has anyone considered this could be more than a simple redirect? Maybe there's a broader strategy we’re not seeing. Like, introducing fans to content they wouldn’t normally click on? Though, I agree, the “Xiaolin Showdown” future show redirect feels like a stretch.

**DigitalDetective: ClickAndScroll**
Checked out both redirects, and yeah, it’s puzzling. I get “Magi-Nation” has its own thing going, but it doesn’t align well with “Monster Allergy.” And hyping a show that’s months away for “Xiaolin Showdown”? Missed opportunity to bridge fans to current content, IMO.

**RetroRewind: PastBlast**
Honestly, it’s kind of sad. Feels like they're quickly moving past “Monster Allergy” and “Xiaolin Showdown” without much of a nod to their legacy. I understand websites update, but this feels more like brushing aside rather than a strategic guide to new favorites.

**ThreadStarter: Anim8Fan**
Really appreciate everyone’s input. It’s clear we all feel there could've been a more thoughtful approach to these redirects. Whether it's guiding fans to similar content or building hype for new shows, the connection should make sense to the fanbase. Here’s hoping Kids WB gets better at this in the future.
Write a follow up saying "Be thankful it's not Will and Dewitt you're being redirected to"

**ThreadStarter: Anim8Fan**
Quick follow-up on our conversation about the odd redirect choices on KidsWB.com for "Monster Allergy" and "Xiaolin Showdown" – Just had a thought that maybe we should look on the bright side. After reading everyone’s perspectives, we’re all a bit bewildered by the choices, but hey, at least we’re not being redirected to “Will and Dewitt.” 😅

**CartoonCollective: ToonsUnited**
Lol, well, when you put it that way, things could definitely be worse. 😂 “Will and Dewitt” is such a leap from “Monster Allergy” and “Xiaolin Showdown,” it's almost comedic to imagine going there expecting our beloved action-packed or supernatural toons.

**AnimationAnalyst: FrameByFrame**
Haha, good point! 😆 Imagining the confusion if "Monster Allergy" fans suddenly found themselves on a page featuring a boy and his talking frog friend. While “Will and Dewitt” has its charm for its intended audience, that’d be an unexpected turn for fans of the departing shows.

**NostalgiaNerd: Y2KCartoonKid**
You got a chuckle out of me with that one. 😁 Be thankful for small mercies, I suppose. Redirects are one thing, but accidentally hyping up toddler-aged fans for “Will and Dewitt” would be a whole other genre of mix-up.

**ToonTalker: LooneyLover42**
Seriously, that comment made my day! 😂 Can't stop picturing the scenario now. It underscores how mismatched those redirects felt, but you’re right, it’s about finding the humor in the situation. At least “Magi-Nation” shares some adventure aspects, unlike a leap to preschool content.

**DigitalDetective: ClickAndScroll**
Imagine the surprise crossover episodes we could’ve had if those redirects were accurate to the spirit of the shows. “Xiaolin Showdown” meets “Will and Dewitt” – now that's a headscratcher! Thanks for lightening the mood, @Anim8Fan. Perspective is everything.

**ThreadStarter: Anim8Fan**
Glad I could add a bit of levity to our discussion! It's important to find the humor amidst changes and surprises, especially in the world of beloved shows and cartoons. Who knows, maybe this will make us all a bit more open to exploring varied content... but yeah, let’s be thankful our nostalgia trips aren’t taking us down the most unexpected paths!
